speaker: patient
text: Doctor, I've been experiencing palpitations for the past few days, and it's making me quite anxious.

speaker: doctor
text: I understand. Let's get to the bottom of this. Have you noticed any other symptoms like chest pain or shortness of breath?

speaker: doctor
text: By the way, your blood glucose level came back at 10.2 mmol/L this morning, which is a bit elevated.

speaker: patient
text: No chest pain, but sometimes I do feel short of breath when climbing stairs.

speaker: doctor
text: Given your symptoms and test results, I am starting you on Lisinopril 10mg once daily to help manage your blood pressure.

speaker: doctor
text: Your primary diagnosis is coronary artery disease, which could be the cause of your palpitations and breathlessness.

speaker: doctor
text: Also, your body temperature is 38.5 degrees Celsius, suggesting you might have an infection or inflammation.

speaker: doctor
text: Your hemoglobin is low at 82 grams per liter, indicating anemia, which might be contributing to your symptoms.

speaker: patient
text: That sounds concerning. Could the palpitations be related to the anemia?

speaker: doctor
text: Yes, anemia can cause your heart to beat faster to compensate for reduced oxygen delivery.

speaker: patient
text: What should I do about the elevated blood glucose? I wasn't aware it was that high.

speaker: doctor
text: We will need to monitor it closely and discuss lifestyle changes. I will also schedule more blood tests soon.

speaker: doctor
text: Interestingly, your repeat blood glucose test shows a lower value of 7.7 mmol/L, which is better than the earlier reading.

speaker: patient
text: Actually, I checked again this morning, and my blood sugar was closer to that lower number you just mentioned.

speaker: doctor
text: Given these findings, I am going to switch you to Amlodipine 10mg once daily instead of Lisinopril to better manage your blood pressure.

speaker: patient
text: Will this new medication affect my heart palpitations?

speaker: doctor
text: Amlodipine helps relax blood vessels and can reduce the workload on your heart, which may improve palpitations.

speaker: doctor
text: By the way, your body temperature has come down slightly to 37.5 degrees Celsius, which is a good sign.

speaker: doctor
text: However, your hemoglobin has dropped a bit further to 80 grams per liter, so we need to investigate the cause of your anemia.

speaker: patient
text: I remember earlier my hemoglobin was at 82 g/L and my temperature was 38.5 °C; it's good to know these are being closely monitored.
